title: Micranthella ciliaris
taxonomy: Micranthella ciliaris is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is further classified into the tribe Astereae. The classification of Micranthella ciliaris into genera is within the subfamily Asteroideae. Within the subfamily, the genus Micranthella is placed. The exact classification within the genus may vary depending on the source.
uses: Micranthella ciliaris is primarily found in the southwestern United States, particularly in Texas. While limited information is available on its medicinal uses, it has been used in traditional medicine by Native American tribes for various purposes. Its leaves and flowers have been used to treat ailments such as fever, coughs, and skin conditions. However, further research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal properties and benefits. intro: Micranthella ciliaris, also known as Bigelow's bitterweed, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Micranthella ciliaris include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are employed to treat a range of conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
